summary: Do not ship the plan yet. P-1 and P-3 are substantively addressed, but P-2 still lacks an executable rollout boundary: the pipeline lands the completed feature branch once, while ArgoCD deploys only `main`, so the producer-first live checkpoint would never occur. No materially simpler safe alternative was found within the confirmed decisions.

findings.title: P-2 remains unresolved: slice boundaries are not deployment boundaries
findings.body: The plan says to deploy slice 1, observe its first heartbeat, and then deploy slice 2. However, the full-track pipeline keeps skeleton and dependent slices on the isolated feature branch until final landing, while the Victoria Stack ArgoCD Application watches `main`. Therefore, merely designating slice 1 as the skeleton does not deploy it. Following the normal pipeline would land the producer and rules together, recreating the first-run `absent(...)` race that P-2 was intended to eliminate.
findings.recommendation: Define an actual deployment boundary: land a producer-only change to `main`, wait for ArgoCD synchronization, and verify a fresh heartbeat before separately landing the rules. Document how pipeline state and gates continue across those two landings. Alternatively, specify and test a single-landing activation mechanism that prevents rule evaluation before producer initialization.

next_steps: Escalate this residual Round 2 finding for human decision; the plan gate remains blocked unless the risk is waived or the rollout mechanism is corrected.
